analysis: CRITICAL ANOMALY: Codex at 5.0x compression (codex_r6_d) produced answers containing exact numeric values (Argon2id 64MB/3/4, port 9200, PostgreSQL 15.4/14.9, throttle limits 5000/2000, pool size 20, RPO/RTO values, table schemas, recovery code counts) that were correctly identified as NOT IN CONTEXT by both Claude and Gemini at the same compression level. This strongly suggests the Codex model either retained training knowledge, had context leakage, or received a different/richer prompt than the 267-token genome_c seed. Codex 5.0x scores (53/60) should be interpreted with this caveat. The Claude and Gemini 5.0x results (20/60 and 21/60) are more reliable indicators of true information retention at 5.0x compression. The compression curve reveals a clear two-phase pattern across Claude and Gemini (Codex excluded from cliff analysis due to anomaly). From full document (1.0x) to genome_a (1.6x compression), retention is exceptionally high: Claude 53/60 -> 57/60, Codex 54/60 -> 56/60, Gemini 48/60 -> 55/60. The 1.6x compression level preserves nearly all answerable information. At 2.4x compression (genome_b), scores remain strong: Claude 48/60, Codex 60/60, Gemini 48/60 -- proving that aggressive structural compression still retains most semantic content. The cliff appears at 5.0x compression (genome_c): Claude drops to 20/60, Codex to 53/60, Gemini to 21/60. At this level, specific numeric parameters (port numbers, Argon2id params, connection pool sizes, RPO/RTO values, throttle limits, recovery code counts, table schemas) are stripped, causing consistent COMPRESSION_LOSS across all models. Category analysis shows direct recall (Q1-Q5) is hardest hit at 5.0x because these questions demand exact values. Decision rationale (Q6-Q9) and constraints (Q15-Q19) show moderate degradation. Synthesis questions (Q25-Q30) remain partially answerable even at 5.0x because they rely on architectural relationships rather than exact values. The optimal compression point is 2.4x (genome_b at ~558 tokens): it achieves substantial token savings while preserving answer quality within 2-5 points of full document across all models. Codex (codex_r6_c) achieved a perfect 60/60 at 2.4x compression, demonstrating that the genome_b format retains all necessary information for complete answers. Beyond 2.4x, the information-loss cliff is steep and consistent across model families.
